title: betula alba f. pubescens
intro: Betula alba f. pubescens, also known as downy birch, is a subspecies of the white birch tree (Betula alba) that is characterized by its hairy or downy appearance. The bark of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are due to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Betula alba f. pubescens include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant include tea, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health issues, making it a valuable resource in natural medicine.
